text.title: Weekly Growth Review Rhythm Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Weekly Growth Review Rhythm application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Pick 5-7 metrics: 1 NSM (north-star), 2-3 leading indicators, 1-2 efficiency, 1 health. More = ignored.
rule.rationale: Cognitive science: 5-7 items is working-memory limit; dashboards with >9 items are skimmed.

rule.statement: Maintain a rolling log of ≥3 active hypotheses with status (in-flight, won, lost, parked). Hypotheses are the unit of growth, not 'campaigns'.
rule.rationale: Without hypothesis structure, growth ops devolves to vibes; explicit log creates compounding.

rule.statement: Each weekly review produces ≥1 decision: continue, kill, double-down, redesign. 'Wait and see' = no decision = drift.
rule.rationale: Without forced decisions, weekly review becomes status report; explicit decision drives velocity.

rule.statement: Review at fixed slot weekly. Same day, same time. Skipping = drift.
rule.rationale: Habit-formation literature; flexible schedule is unkept schedule.

rule.statement: Quarterly: are these still the right 5-7 metrics? Promote, demote, retire. Static metric sets blind teams to new realities.
rule.rationale: Stage of business shifts metric relevance; quarterly revisit keeps the dashboard alive.
